docket_entries.description: MONEY CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF FOR $7,500.00 FILED BY LEE, RAMIND AS TO DEFENDANT VONG, QUAY ORDER SETTING TRIAL ISSUED FOR JAN-12-2011 AT 08:30 AM IN DEPT 506

SMALL CLAIMS CALENDAR OF JAN-12-2011 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF JAN-12-2011 BY DONALD S. MITCHELL. APPEARANCE BY PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT WAIVES UNTIMELY SERVICE IN OPEN COURT. CLERK: S. VANTREE. NOT REPORTED. (506)
SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF JAN-12-2011 - JUDGMENT.

PARTIES WAIVE SERVICE ON PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF LEE, RAMIND RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT VONG, QUAY $7,500.00 PRINCIPAL, $75.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $7,575.00 STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED
PROOF OF SERVICE FILED ON CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF SERVED JAN-02-2011, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT VONG, QUAY
NOTICE OF APPEAL FILED BY APPELLANT VONG, QUAY MAILED TO RESPONDENT LEE, RAMIND
CASE_TRANSFERRED TO SUPERIOR COURT, RECORD AND EXHIBITS TRANSFERRED
TRANSMITTAL RECEIPT FILED (RECEIVED EXHIBITS FROM SMALL CLAIMS DIVISION)
SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE HEARING SET FOR MAR-16-2011 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 414
MINI-MINUTES FOR MAR-16-2011 9:00 AM FOR DEPT 414
STIPULATION TO USE JUDGE PRO TEM AND ORDER FILED BY VONG, QUAY LEE, RAMIND
SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL CALENDAR OF MAR-16-2011 - JUDGMENT ENTERED. CLERK - LESLIE GOMEZ, NOTHING ON RECORD, DEPT. 414
THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT APPELLANT VONG, QUAY TAKE NOTHING FROM RESPONDENT LEE, RAMIND NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT AND CERTIFICATE OF MAILING FILED
RECORD REMANDED TO SMALL CLAIMS (EXHIBITS RETURNED TO SMALL CLAIMS DIVISION)

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Mar-16-2011 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: 9:47am
Judge Pro Tem Guy Kornblum, clerk Leslie Gomez, nothing on record, dept. 414.
Cause called for Small Claims Appeal. Appellant, Quay Vong appears with and through her attorney Dennis Carr. Respondent, Ramind Lee appears in pro per.
Parties, previously sworn, testify in the narrative and respond to questions from Judge Kornblum.
